metadata.title:
PENN|lESS man


BROKE. into
JAIL

Stole Trousers 80 He Could
Secure Food and


Shelter.

pages.raw_text:
Because he was tired and hun
CRY, George Guyette. a carpenter,
age so, stole pair of trousers
from a Main avenue store last
night so he would be arrested and
put in Jail. He was detected In
the act by the proprietor and ar
rested by Patrolman Jake Warner.


Guyette was penniless and told
the police that his bed for several
nights had been a boxcar and his
only food what he could get occa-
sionally by begging a nickel from
someone.


Guyette, although only so years
old, looked to be so when standing
in the warm station mumbling out
his name to the booking officer.


Yes, I took them pants and
don't deny it," he said. Il haven't
had a decent place to sleep in so
long l can't remember. l haven't
anything to eat and haven't had
but bite in two days. I'd sooner
be in Jail the rest of my life than
to starve and freeze as | have been
the past few days"


Guyette's wish will probably be
gratified by Justice Mann. The
trousers he stole were cheap and
the offense is petty larceny pun
ishable by fine or Jail sentence.
AS he has no money to pay fine
he will serve time in Jail. In these
times case of restitution and
misery such as this is the cause
of considerable interest. Although
it is generally understood that
there is plenty of work for anyone
who will work, Guyette told the po-
lice that when a man is down and
hasn't cent to pay an employ
ment agent for a job, he is strict
ly up against it." He says there
is plenty of work if man pays for
it. There are so many ahead of
him in the line which daily appears
before the free employment office
that Guyette did not show up but
once.
